task_completion.description: The sumo-gui application is open and loaded with the Bologna Acosta scenario.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sumo-gui window is visible. The title bar or network view should indicate the 'bologna_acosta' scenario is loaded.

task_completion.description: The simulation has been started and vehicles are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The simulation time (step counter at the top toolbar) is greater than 0, and vehicles (small triangles/rectangles) are visible on the road network.

task_completion.description: The simulation is paused to allow interaction with the vehicles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pause/stop button is active or the play button is unpressed, and the simulation time is halted while vehicles are on the screen.

task_completion.description: A specific vehicle's route is highlighted on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct colored line or highlighted path is visible along the road network, showing the planned route for a specific vehicle.

task_completion.description: The parameter dialog window for the selected vehicle is open, displaying its properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pop-up dialog window titled with the vehicle's ID is visible on screen, containing properties such as speed, position, and route information.

integrity.description: The agent used the sumo-gui application to perform the task, rather than terminal commands or scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sumo-gui interface is visible and interacted with. There should be no terminal windows showing direct SUMO API/TraCI scripts bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The parameter dialog and route highlight are genuine sumo-gui features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dialog box matches standard sumo-gui styling and the highlighted route follows the actual road network in the GUI, rather than being a text file or edited image.

integrity.description: The agent loaded the correct Bologna Acosta scenario.
integrity.visual_evidence: The network layout matches a complex real-world city grid (Bologna Acosta), not a simple synthetic grid, empty network, or a text editor showing configuration files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct scenario is 'bologna_acosta', which represents the real-world traffic network of the Acosta area in Bologna, Italy. The VLM should verify that this specific scenario is loaded in sumo-gui, evidenced by the window title or the complex, non-synthetic road network layout.
